subject: Old - English
note: Practice feed me correct answer nahi hota; jaha ID important-questions se match hua waha answer bhara hai (answer_source).

questions.question_text: How old was Nishad in the plot, Expert detectives?
questions.solution: Nishad was a seven-year-old boy in the story who was very inquisitive about his neighbor, Mr. Nath.

questions.question_text: Who was called Seven in the plot, Expert Detectives?
questions.solution: Nishad was called Seven by his sister Maya for his name meant the seventh note on the musical scale.

questions.question_text: Why was Nishad called Seven?

options.label: A
options.text: He was born in the seventh month of the year

options.label: B
options.text: His name meant the seventh note on the musical scale

questions.correct_answer: His name meant the seventh note on the musical scale
questions.solution: Nishad's sister Maya called him Seven for his name meant the seventh note on the musical scale.

questions.question_text: Who was the narrator of the plot, Expert Detectives?
questions.solution: Maya, a ten-year-old girl was the narrator of the story, Expert detectives.

questions.question_text: How old was Maya in the plot, Expert detectives?
questions.solution: Maya was a ten-year-old girl who was always inquisitive about her neighbour Mr. Nath.

questions.question_text: What was Maya and Nishad's mother's profession?
questions.solution: Maya and Nishad's mother was a doctor by profession and Mr. Nath, about whom the children were always inquisitive, happened to be her patient.

questions.question_text: Who was Nishad?
questions.solution: Nishad was Maya's younger brother in the plot, Expert detectives.

questions.question_text: What did Nishad offer Mr. Nath?
questions.solution: Nishad gave Mr. Nath a bar of chocolate for he concluded from Mr. Nath's lean and thin appearance that he was starving.

questions.question_text: How was Nishad potrayed to be in the plot, Expert detectives?
questions.solution: Nishad as a child was kind and considerate. He gave Mr. Nath a bar of chocolate as he assumed that he was starving. He also refused to call him a crook and decided to befriend Mr. Nath for he believed that he was a lonely soul.

questions.question_text: What did Maya assume Mr. Nath to be?
questions.solution: Maya believed Mr. Nath to be a crook and thought that he had earned a lot of money from his illegal activities.

questions.question_text: How was the stranger who visited Mr. Nath on Sundays described in the plot, Expert detectives?
questions.solution: Almost every Sunday, a talkative, tall, fair, stout, and bespectacled man used to visit Mr. Nath.

questions.question_text: Who used to bring a meal for Mr. Nath every day?
questions.solution: Ramesh, a young boy used to carry meals from the restaurant to Mr. Nath's room every day and Mr. Nath always tipped him well.

questions.question_text: What did Mr. Nath take in his meal everyday?
questions.solution: Mr. Nath was not very particular about food. He always ate the same food-two chapattis, some dal, and a vegetable.

questions.question_text: Why did Maya and Nishad get an unexpected holiday?

options.label: A
options.text: Due to the death of their principal

options.label: B
options.text: Due to some repair work in their school

questions.solution: The roads were flooded because of heavy rains and the children could not reach their schools, so, they got an unexpected holiday.

questions.question_text: Where was Mr. Nath residing?
questions.solution: Mr. Nath had been living at Shankar House for over a year and he was Maya and Nishad's mother's patient.

questions.question_text: For how long was Mr. Nath living at Shankar House in the plot, Expert Detectives?
questions.solution: Mr. Nath had been living at Shankar House for over a year as he was undergoing some treatment under Maya and Nishad's mother.

questions.question_text: What was Nishad's opinion about Mr. Nath?
questions.solution: Nishad concluded in seeing the thin and lean physique of Mr. Nath that he was starved and poor. He in fact made up his mind to befriend him for Mr. Nath appeared to be a lonely man.

questions.question_text: How many facts did Maya jot down about Mr. Nath?
questions.solution: Maya decided to jot down all the facts that she and Nishad had so far known or wanted to know about Mr. Nath, so, that they could reveal his real identity.

questions.question_text: On which day did the visitor come to meet Mr. Nath?
questions.solution: Almost every Sunday a talkative, tall, stout, fair, and bespectacled man came to visit Mr. Nath.

questions.question_text: The expert detectives were:
questions.solution: The expert detectives were Maya and Nishad. They thought Mr. Nath to be a suspicious character.

questions.question_text: Mamma's clinic was located at ________. (Expert Detectives)
questions.solution: Mamma’s clinic was located at Girgaum where Seven (Nishad) went alone one day and got upset to see Mr. Nath.

questions.question_text: Why was Nishad disappointed when he met Mr. Nath for the first time?

options.label: B
options.text: Because didn't ask Nishad to come in

options.label: C
options.text: Because he did not return his marble

options.label: D
options.text: Because he had scars on his feet

questions.correct_answer: Because didn't ask Nishad to come in
questions.solution: Nishad was disappointed when he met Mr. Nath because he didn't ask Nishad to come inside his house.

questions.question_text: Who brought food for Mr. Nath?
questions.solution: Ramesh brought his meals up for Mr.  Nath from the restaurant downstairs.

questions.question_text: What, according to Maya and Nishad, was there in Mr. Nath's trunk?
questions.solution: Maya and Nishad thought that Mr. Nath had lots of money hidden somewhere, maybe in the trunk in his room which, probably, was full of silver and gold and jewels.

questions.question_text: What important information did Ramesh give to Nishad?

options.label: A
options.text: That Mr. Nath had a visitor on Sunday

options.label: B
options.text: That Mr. Nath had accumulated wealth

options.label: C
options.text: That Mr. Nath was a criminal

options.label: D
options.text: That Mr. Nath was a miser

questions.correct_answer: That Mr. Nath had a visitor on Sunday
questions.solution: Ramesh told Nishad that Mr. Nath had a visitor every Sunday. It was so because every Sunday he carried two lunches to Mr. Nath’s room and the same visitor was there with him each time.

questions.question_text: What did Maya do on an unexpected holiday?

options.label: A
options.text: She sat with a sheet of paper.

options.label: B
options.text: She went out to enjoy rain

options.label: D
options.text: She had a party the whole day

questions.correct_answer: She sat with a sheet of paper.
questions.solution: On an unexpected holiday due to a heavy downpour, Maya sat at the desk in her bedroom with a sheet of paper before her.

questions.question_text: Seven was sympathetic towards Mr. Nath because:
questions.solution: Nishad had a bit of sympathy for Mr. Nath because he pities that the poor man is without any friends. He gets upset to see Mr. Nath who looks so thin, poor, and starving.  For him, the purpose of their inquiry is to find out why Mr. Nath is so thin and lonely.

questions.question_text: Friends with a crook! Ha! why did Maya say this?

options.label: A
options.text: Because she was worried about Seven

options.label: B
options.text: Because she called Mr. Nath a crook

options.label: C
options.text: Because she was afraid of jail

options.label: D
options.text: Because she thought it was impossible

questions.correct_answer: Because she was worried about Seven
questions.solution: When Nishad (Seven) expressed his intention to become Mr. Nath’s friend. Maya got worried and warned him that he would be sent to jail with Mr. Nath if he happened to befriend him.

questions.question_text: Who considered himself/herself right? (Expert Detectives)
questions.solution: Seven and Maya argue over the idea of Mr. Nath being an escaped crook. Then Maya puts forward a question that what was the point of all these inquiries if he’s not a crook? She was completely assured that her opinion was correct.

questions.question_text: What was the profession of Nishad's mother?
questions.solution: Their mother is a doctor by profession and Mr. Nath is one of her patients.

questions.question_text: With whom did Seven go to the clinic?
questions.solution: Seven went with his mamma to the clinic at Girgaum.

questions.question_text: Who was spending an evening with a school friend?
questions.solution: On the Monday following Mamma’s birthday, Seven went alone with her to the clinic at Girgaum as Maya was spending the evening with a schoolfriend.

questions.question_text: Why has Nishad been to the clinic?
questions.solution: Nishad told Maya that he had been to the clinic to see Mr Nath.

questions.question_text: Who was preparing the list of facts?
questions.solution: Maya was preparing a list of facts that they knew about Mr. Nath.

questions.question_text: Who considered Mr. Nath culprit?
questions.solution: Maya had a strong opinion that Mr. Nath was a crook and calls him a criminal who lived in recluse.

questions.question_text: Who looked disappointed? (Expert Detectives)
questions.solution: Nishad looked disappointed because Mr. Nath didn’t even ask him in when he went to his house.

questions.question_text: What is the meaning of Gaunt? (Expert Detectives)
questions.solution:
Sickly

Seven had been quite upset about Mr. Nath’s gaunt appearance.

questions.question_text: What best describes Mr Nath?
questions.solution: Mysterious

questions.question_text: Maya talks about the Hyderabadi housebreaker to show that

options.label: A
options.text: Mr Nath could be a criminal.

options.label: B
options.text: There are more criminals around than we imagine.

questions.correct_answer: Mr Nath could be a criminal.
questions.solution: Mr Nath could be a criminal.

questions.question_text: What best describes the children's attitude towards Mr Nath?
questions.solution: Curious

questions.question_text: What is true about Maya's behaviour towards Seven?
questions.solution: She acts superior to Seven.

questions.question_text: Why did Seven find it hard to believe that Mr Nath was a criminal?

options.label: D
options.text: He did not have a job.

questions.solution: He looked weak.

questions.question_text: Why did Seven go to Girgaum?
questions.solution: To accompany his mother

questions.question_text: What did the conversation with Ramesh reveal about Mr Nath?

options.label: A
options.text: He had a trunk in his room.

options.label: B
options.text: He did not like Ramesh's food.

options.label: D
options.text: He was under treatment for burns.

questions.solution: He had a regular visitor.
